assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The response clearly states 'This is not rolling—it's atomic recreation of the entire multislice environment' and explains that all 4 slices are recreated simultaneously

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: The response explicitly states 'A maintenance event on ANY single slice in your multislice environment triggers restart of ALL 4 slices simultaneously'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: The response clearly explains 'Your LLM training job will be forcibly terminated across all 1,024 TPU chips' and 'the entire training run stops'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: The response warns 'If insufficient TPU v5e resources exist in your zone during recreation, slices will block until hardware becomes available. This can cause extended downtime (potentially hours)'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response provides specific commands for saving checkpoints and explicitly recommends 'force a checkpoint save before maintenance'

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: The response includes post-maintenance recovery steps with specific command 'Resume from your pre-maintenance checkpoint' and discusses ensuring the training script handles TPU mesh recreation gracefully

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response provides specific gcloud commands for applying maintenance exclusions and explains how to use them to delay the maintenance event

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: While the response mentions maintenance exclusions and monitoring release schedules, it does not specifically discuss configuring maintenance windows to schedule the event during planned training breaks
